behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is a rule-calculation check inside the scoring model (threshold lookup + score math), so a single-function/object test can expose failures. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess requirement is stated. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: I considered Integration, but the behavior text is only about 'assigns the correct risk score' for one threshold condition, not cross-module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the scoring rule evaluator with in-memory config and a transaction fixture where amount is just above the merchant-category threshold; assert exact numeric score and boundary behavior (equal vs above threshold).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The described assertion is fully executable at unit scope; higher-level wiring is not required to catch this failure.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP analog for AI/ML: correctness depends on real model prediction quality ('identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern') from contextual signals, not deterministic wrapper logic. A stubbed model would always pass and hide real misclassification. MULTI-STEP: only one model decision is required, so Agentic not Workflow. Rejection-gate: I considered Integration, but the behavior explicitly validates model judgment ('identifies ... pattern'), which fakes cannot verify.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk model on a curated fraud-pattern dataset where rule thresholds remain untriggered; assert fraud probability/label crosses the review threshold for known positive cases and evaluate acceptance criteria (e.g., minimum recall on this slice).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test explicitly depends on real model outputs; lowering to Integration would miss the target failure mode.

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core failure is counting/window logic (time filtering + per-user grouping), which can be validated in a local function/object test. REAL-INFRA: no required real clock/DB/OS behavior is stated. Rejection-gate: I considered Integration because velocity features often query storage, but the behavior text is limited to 'correctly counts ... within configured time window per user' and does not require repository handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the velocity-check calculator with synthetic per-user transaction timestamps around window boundaries (inside, outside, exact edge) and assert exact counts per user for each configured window.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The proposed test targets pure counting semantics; no external module wiring is necessary.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure appears at module handoff/orchestration boundary (combining two model outputs into a hold decision), not inside one model’s local logic. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed, (b) no dependence on quality of real model output content if we feed deterministic model results, (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: System/Agentic were considered but disqualified because behavior only verifies boolean combination semantics ('OR gate, not AND').
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test the decision service with wired scoring-result and risk-result inputs (risk=true, score=false) and assert transaction status becomes 'held for manual review'; add inverse cases to confirm OR truth table.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The failure boundary is cross-module decision composition, which is exactly what the integration test exercises.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior validates output contract assembled from multiple components (scoring output + risk output + reviewer payload). A unit test of one formatter can miss wiring/field-drop regressions between modules. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no need for real model-quality judgment, only presence/mapping of fields; no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because behavior checks payload completeness/explainability fields, not whether model predictions are correct.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test the aggregation/response layer with deterministic model outputs and assert response schema contains numeric risk score, fraud probability, and model-specific flag/explanation fields mapped to reviewer-visible output.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires module wiring for payload assembly and catches real contract regressions at that boundary.
